title: PostgreSQL Work Claims, SKIP LOCKED, Leases, and Fencing · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs postgresql work claims, skip locked, leases, and fencing from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Claim a bounded due batch with ordered FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED work in a short transaction and release locks before network I/O.
Issue attempt and fencing tokens with owner, expiry, heartbeat budget, and state so stale workers cannot commit.
Finalize success or failure only when source, attempt, token, and current lease match in one affected-row check.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 3078 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test crash after claim, expiry during fetch, duplicate claim, heartbeat loss, database failover, cancellation, and stale completion. Artifact under review: multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 4315 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test crash after claim, expiry during fetch, duplicate claim, heartbeat loss, database failover, cancellation, and stale completion.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, keep Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test crash after claim, expiry during fetch, duplicate claim, heartbeat loss, database failover, cancellation, and stale completion.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 4315 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove short SKIP LOCKED claims, fenced leases, conditional finalize, and fault recovery; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test crash after claim, expiry during fetch, duplicate claim, heartbeat loss, database failover, cancellation, and stale completion.

For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Lease Fault Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Two workers never overlap when SKIP LOCKED is present.
